Skip to the comment form
Preview only. This is an accessible HTML preview of the WCAG 2.1 AA Drupal Webform that replaces the current ArcGIS Survey123 form. Submissions on this page do not enter the city's records — use the live city portal to submit your comment. See the bundle README for install and import.
Language:

HOME-ARP Allocation Plan · Public Comment

HOME-ARP Allocation Plan — Public Comment

Submit a public comment on the City of Colorado Springs HOME-ARP Allocation Plan amendment. Comment period is April 24 through May 26, 2026.

About this form

The City of Colorado Springs is collecting public comment on a proposed amendment to its HOME-ARP Allocation Plan. The public comment period is open from April 24 through May 26, 2026.

You can submit comments through this form, or by any of the following methods. All methods enter the public record on equal footing.

  • Email: CityHousingAndCommunityVitality@coloradosprings.gov
  • Phone: 719-385-5859 (TTY users may dial 711 for relay)
  • Postal mail: Housing and Homelessness Response, 30 S. Nevada Ave., Colorado Springs, CO 80903
  • In person: at any scheduled public hearing during the comment period

If you need this form, the draft amendment, or any city document in another format (large print, Braille, ASL, audio, plain language, or another language), tell us in the Accessibility request section at the bottom of this form, or contact the Office of Accessibility at 719-385-5169.

All fields except your comment are optional. We collect contact information only to follow up if you ask us to.

Please fix the items below before submitting:
    Thank you. Your comment has been received.

    A confirmation will be emailed to you if you provided an email address. The full submission is retained in the public record.

    Fields marked with an asterisk are required.

    About you

    All fields in this section are optional.

    Provide a name only if you are comfortable having it associated with your comment in the public record.

    We will only use this to follow up if you ask us to.

    Optional. Include if you would like a phone follow-up.

    Helps us understand which neighborhoods are weighing in.

    I am submitting this comment as

    Choose the description that best fits.

    Only if you are commenting on behalf of an organization.

    What is your comment about?

    Select all that apply. This helps us route your comment to the right team.

    Topics
    Your comment

    Please share your comment, question, or suggestion. There is no length limit, but more focused comments are easier to act on.

    0 characters

    Attach a letter, data, or supporting document. Accepted formats: PDF, Word, plain text, JPG, PNG. Maximum 10 MB.

    10 MB maximum · PDF, DOC, DOCX, ODT, TXT, RTF, JPG, PNG

    Follow-up

    If checked, the team may reach out using the contact information you provided.

    Occasional updates from the Housing and Homelessness Response Department. You can unsubscribe at any time.

    Provide an email or phone above to enable follow-up options.

    Accessibility request

    If you need this form, the draft plan, or city documents in another format, tell us here. The Office of Accessibility will follow up.

    For example: large print, Braille, ASL interpretation, plain-language summary, Spanish translation, audio, or any other accommodation.

    Privacy notice. Comments submitted through this form become part of the public record for the HOME-ARP Allocation Plan amendment. Personal information you provide (name, contact details) is used only to follow up if you ask us to. Your comment will be summarized in the city's response to public comment, which is published with the final plan. The full submission is retained by the Housing and Homelessness Response Department in accordance with the city's records retention schedule.

    Accessible HTML preview of the Drupal Webform replacement · WCAG 2.1 AA · Bilingual EN/ES · Companion to bundle-readme.html
